q: Where did God speak to Moses from the burning bush?
e: Exodus 3:1 — Moses came to Horeb, the mountain of God.

q: What was the first plague God sent upon Egypt?
e: Exodus 7:20 — All the water in the Nile was changed into blood.

q: How many plagues did God send upon Egypt in total?
e: Exodus 7-12 — There were ten plagues starting with blood and ending with the death of the firstborn.

q: What was the final plague?
e: Exodus 11:5 — Every firstborn son in Egypt will die.

q: Through which body of water did the Israelites cross on dry land?
e: Exodus 14:21-22 — The Lord drove the sea back... and the Israelites went through the sea on dry ground.

q: What food did God provide from heaven for the Israelites in the wilderness?
e: Exodus 16:14-15 — When the dew was gone, thin flakes like frost appeared... they called it manna.

q: On what mountain did Moses receive the Ten Commandments?
e: Exodus 19:20 — The Lord descended to the top of Mount Sinai and called Moses.

q: What was the first commandment?
o: You shall have no other gods before me
e: Exodus 20:3 — You shall have no other gods before me.

q: Who was Moses' brother?
e: Exodus 4:14 — Is there not your brother, Aaron the Levite?

q: What did Aaron make for the people to worship while Moses was on the mountain?
e: Exodus 32:4 — He took what they handed him and made it into an idol cast in the shape of a calf.

q: Who was Moses' sister?
e: Exodus 15:20 — Then Miriam the prophet, Aaron’s sister, took a timbrel in her hand.

q: What was the name of the portable tent where God's presence dwelt?
e: Exodus 25-27 — God gave instructions for building the Tabernacle.

q: How long did the Israelites wander in the wilderness?
e: Exodus 16:35 — The Israelites ate manna forty years.

q: What was the name of Moses' wife?
e: Exodus 2:21 — Moses agreed to stay with the man, who gave his daughter Zipporah to Moses.

q: Who was the father-in-law of Moses?
e: Exodus 3:1 — Moses was tending the flock of Jethro his father-in-law.

q: What did Moses' staff turn into when he threw it on the ground?
e: Exodus 4:3 — Moses threw it on the ground and it became a snake.

q: What did the Israelites eat on the night of the first Passover?
e: Exodus 12:8 — That same night they are to eat the meat roasted... along with bitter herbs and bread made without yeast.

q: Why did Moses' mother hide him in a basket in the Nile?
o: To save him from Pharaoh's order to kill Hebrew baby boys
To hide him from his sister
To help him find Pharaoh's daughter
e: Exodus 2:2-3 — When she could hide him no longer, she got a papyrus basket...

q: Who found baby Moses in the river?
e: Exodus 2:5 — Pharaoh’s daughter went down to the Nile to bathe... she saw the basket among the reeds.

q: What was the name of Moses' son?
e: Exodus 2:22 — Zipporah gave birth to a son, and Moses named him Gershom.

q: How many years were the Israelites in Egypt?
e: Exodus 12:40 — Now the length of time the Israelite people lived in Egypt was 430 years.

q: Which plague involved insects that covered the land?
e: Exodus 8:16-17 — All the dust throughout the land of Egypt became gnats.

q: What did the Israelites have to put on their doorposts to be spared from the final plague?
e: Exodus 12:7 — They are to take some of the blood and put it on the sides and tops of the doorframes.

q: Which tribe did Moses and Aaron belong to?
e: Exodus 2:1 — Now a man of the tribe of Levi married a Levite woman.

q: How did God lead the Israelites by day?
e: Exodus 13:21 — By day the Lord went ahead of them in a pillar of cloud.

q: How did God lead the Israelites by night?
e: Exodus 13:21 — By night [the Lord went ahead of them] in a pillar of fire to give them light.

q: Who was Moses' successor who led the people into the Promised Land?
e: Exodus 17:9, Joshua 1 — Joshua was Moses' aide and led the battle against the Amalekites.

q: What did Moses do to make the bitter water at Marah sweet?
o: He threw a piece of wood into it
He struck it with his staff
e: Exodus 15:25 — Then the Lord showed him a piece of wood. He threw it into the water, and the water became fit to drink.

q: What did Moses have to do to ensure the Israelites won the battle against the Amalekites?
e: Exodus 17:11 — As long as Moses held up his hands, the Israelites were winning.

q: Who helped Moses by holding up his arms during the battle?
e: Exodus 17:12 — Aaron and Hur held his hands up—one on one side, one on the other.

q: What was inside the Ark of the Covenant?
e: Exodus 25:16, Hebrews 9:4 — The Ark contained the tablets, manna, and Aaron's rod.

q: What was the name of the place where Moses struck a rock to get water?
e: Exodus 17:7 — He called the place Massah and Meribah because the Israelites quarreled.

q: Who was the primary craftsman for the Tabernacle?
e: Exodus 31:1-2 — I have chosen Bezalel son of Uri... and I have filled him with the Spirit of God.

q: What happened to Moses' face after he spoke with God on the mountain?
e: Exodus 34:29 — He was not aware that his face was radiant because he had spoken with the Lord.

q: How many of the Ten Commandments were about our relationship with God?
e: Exodus 20:1-11 — The first four commandments deal with God; the last six deal with others.

q: What was the punishment for the Israelites' worship of the golden calf?
o: They were sent back to Egypt
e: Exodus 32:28 — The Levites did as Moses commanded, and that day about three thousand of the people died.

q: Who did God appoint to be Moses' spokesman?
e: Exodus 4:14-16 — He will speak to the people for you.

q: What was the tenth commandment?
e: Exodus 20:17 — You shall not covet your neighbor’s house.

q: Which plague came immediately after the gnats?
e: Exodus 8:20-24 — The fourth plague was the plague of flies.

q: What happened to Pharaoh's army when they chased Israel into the sea?
e: Exodus 14:28 — The water flowed back and covered the chariots and horsemen—the entire army of Pharaoh.

q: How often were the Israelites to gather manna?
e: Exodus 16:4 — The people are to go out each day and gather enough for that day.

q: What happened to manna if it was kept until the next morning (except on Friday)?
e: Exodus 16:20 — However, some of them paid no attention... it was full of maggots and began to smell.

q: What was the eighth plague?
e: Exodus 10:4 — I will bring locusts into your country tomorrow.

q: What was the seventh plague?
e: Exodus 9:18 — I will send the worst hailstorm that has ever fallen on Egypt.

q: How many years old was Moses when he first stood before Pharaoh?
e: Exodus 7:7 — Moses was eighty years old and Aaron eighty-three when they spoke to Pharaoh.

q: What did the Israelites use for the gold of the golden calf?
o: Gold they found in the desert
e: Exodus 32:2-3 — Aaron answered them, 'Take off the gold earrings...' So all the people took off their earrings.

q: Which commandment requires honoring your parents?
e: Exodus 20:12 — Honor your father and your mother, so that you may live long.

q: Where did Moses flee to after killing an Egyptian?
e: Exodus 2:15 — Moses fled from Pharaoh and went to live in Midian.

q: What was the name of the assistant to Bezalel in building the Tabernacle?
e: Exodus 31:6 — Moreover, I have appointed Oholiab son of Ahisamak... to help him.

q: How long did the plague of darkness last?
e: Exodus 10:22-23 — Total darkness covered all Egypt for three days.
